posts.name: Barry Rothof
posts.cooked:
2011 Clos Rougeard (Foucault) Saumur-Champigny Les Poyeux

Wonderful expressive nose. On the palate silky textured, with excellent balance of fine, ripe tannins, acid freshness and blackcurrant fruit, ending with intensity and finesse. A Les Amoureuses-like experience!!

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: Had one of these last week, similar experience. I actually thought it seemed younger and edgier that one I had a couple of years ago. Time will do this wine good, I think.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: Hmmm maybe I’ll take down my offer to sell on commerce corner!! Ha

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
IMHO, it’s a very good but not necessarily great Rougeard, but very solid for the vintage, definitely best of class for the vintage. Had it last year with a couple Berserkers:

WineBerserkers – 7 Apr 17

Orlando Berserker Night w/Jamet, La La, Rougeard, '65 Colares and other Goodies

The Orlando Berserker crew - a whopping total of three of us - got together with my normal wine group, mostly my cycling buddies, for another exceptional night. You guys know my little buddy MarcF lives here, but Charlie Carnes you may not know,...

I’ll sit on the 2011 Les P and the Bourg for a handful more years. They should be striking.

Fu, sell it, you won’t like it like you do those Rhones! Or trade me, as my yak palate loves this stuff.

link_counts.title: Orlando Berserker Night w/Jamet, La La, Rougeard, '65 Colares and other Goodies - WINE TALK - WineBerserkers

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Karl K:

What is a yak palate?

People that like green, thin, weedy wines.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: The '11 Poyeaux is a big step up over the Clos, but I struggle to believe this will ever rank as a great vintage for Clos Rougeard. I’m not even sure that they made the red wines of the vintage in the Loire that year. But hey, it’s a very nice vintage for the Brézé.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
William Kelley:

The '11 Poyeaux is a big step up over the Clos, but I struggle to believe this will ever rank as a great vintage for Clos Rougeard. I’m not even sure that they made the red wines of the vintage in the Loire that year. But hey, it’s a very nice vintage for the Brézé.

Curious who you think did. I must admit that I am also very partial to the 2011 Franc de Pieds by Baudry and Plouzeau.

posts.name: CHIU LIN
posts.cooked: I had a delicious ‘11 Le Bourg last month while on holiday in Sydney, wonderful wine, nearly as good the ‘08 version.
